title: +
content:
+
+Public outputs undergo fact checking, bias detection and cultural analysis before being delivered. Decisions are logged to an immutable audit ledger, ensuring accountability.
+
+## Formal Foundations and Representations
+
+### Category Theory and Graph Grammars
+
+RLOS relies on **categorical semiotics**. Linguistic units at various scales are treated as objects; transformations between them are morphisms, and functors map structures across layers or modalities【3†L145-L165】. Natural transformations express systematic meaning relationships. The system uses **multimodal graph grammars** to represent syntactic dependencies, semantic networks, rhetorical hypergraphs and cross‑modal links【3†L145-L165】.
+
+### Semiotic Lattices and Linguistic Tensors
+
+Conceptual lattices organise meaning through subsumption relations; attributes represent semantic features and objects represent expressions【3†L165-L185】. High‑dimensional **linguistic tensors** encode diachronic, geographic, social and modal variation【3†L165-L185】. A domain‑specific **Rhetorical Visualization Language (RVL)** stores rhetorical structures (claims, evidence, warrants, backings and rebuttals) along with ethos/pathos/logos markers【3†L165-L185】.
